meta.title: Web Audio API Best Practices for SOTY Event Horizon
meta.author: Cleanlystudio
meta.description: Comprehensive Web Audio API database for procedural sound design in immersive WebGL experiences

audio_context_lifecycle.description: AudioContext is the main entry point for all Web Audio operations

user_gesture_requirement.description: Browsers require a user gesture (click, tap, keypress) before the AudioContext can produce sound
implementation: Create AudioContext in a suspended state
On first user interaction: audioCtx.resume()
Show a 'Click to enable audio' or 'Start experience' button
Alternatively, create AudioContext inside a click event handler

browsers.chrome: Requires user gesture since Chrome 66 (2018). Context starts suspended.
browsers.safari: Requires user gesture. webkitAudioContext may be needed for older Safari.
browsers.firefox: Requires user gesture since Firefox 66. Less strict than Chrome in some cases.

states.suspended: Created but not processing audio. No CPU usage.
states.running: Actively processing audio. Uses CPU/GPU resources.
states.closed: Permanently stopped. Cannot be resumed. Resources released.

best_practices: Create one AudioContext per application — don't create multiples
Resume on user gesture, suspend when tab is not visible (Page Visibility API)
Close when the experience is completely finished (navigation away)
Check audioCtx.state before operations: if (audioCtx.state === 'suspended') audioCtx.resume()

node_graph.description: Web Audio uses a graph of connected audio nodes: source → effects → destination
node_graph.complex_chain: OscillatorNode → BiquadFilterNode → ConvolverNode → DynamicsCompressorNode → GainNode → AnalyserNode → AudioContext.destination
node_graph.disconnection: node.disconnect() — important for cleanup and preventing memory leaks
node_graph.parallel_chains: Multiple sources can connect to the same destination. Multiple effects can be in parallel with GainNodes for mix control.

audio_worklets.description: Custom audio processing in a dedicated audio thread (replacement for deprecated ScriptProcessorNode)

processor_file.description: Runs in AudioWorkletGlobalScope — separate thread from main thread

audio_worklets.use_cases: Custom synthesis, granular synthesis, complex procedural audio that built-in nodes can't handle
audio_worklets.event_horizon_use: Shepard tone generator, time dilation audio processing, custom gravitational frequency modulation

thread_model.main_thread: Node creation, connection, parameter scheduling, buffer loading
thread_model.audio_thread: Sample-by-sample processing, AudioWorklet execution, built-in node processing
thread_model.rule: NEVER do heavy computation on the audio thread (AudioWorklet). It runs at audio rate (44100 or 48000 Hz). Any stutter = audio glitch.
thread_model.communication: Main → Audio: AudioParam.setValueAtTime(), port.postMessage(). Audio → Main: port.postMessage().

sine.description: Pure tone. Single frequency. No harmonics.
sine.use: Sub-bass drones, pure tones, mixing basis, test tones
sine.event_horizon: Sub-bass (20-40Hz) for the black hole's presence. Pure tone for singularity emergence.

square.event_horizon: Data sonification clicks, digital HUD sounds

sawtooth.use: Basis for subtractive synthesis, pads (when filtered), lead sounds
sawtooth.event_horizon: Filtered sawtooth for evolving pad sounds, tension building

triangle.description: Odd harmonics at reduced amplitude. Softer than square.
triangle.use: Soft pads, gentle tones, woodwind-like sounds

custom.description: Define arbitrary waveform via Fourier coefficients using PeriodicWave
custom.use: Any sound that doesn't fit standard waveforms. Organ-like tones, bell-like tones.

additive_synthesis.description: Build complex sounds by combining multiple sine oscillators at different frequencies and amplitudes
additive_synthesis.technique: Create N OscillatorNodes with frequencies at harmonic multiples. Mix with individual GainNodes.
additive_synthesis.event_horizon_use: Build rich drone sounds from harmonic series. Evolve harmonic content over time (scroll position controls harmonic amplitudes).

subtractive_synthesis.description: Start with harmonically rich waveform (sawtooth, square), remove frequencies with filters
subtractive_synthesis.technique: Automate filter cutoff frequency to create sweeping, evolving sounds
subtractive_synthesis.event_horizon_use: Filtered sweeps for transitions, evolving textures during approach

fm_synthesis.description: Frequency Modulation — one oscillator (modulator) modulates the frequency of another (carrier)
implementation: const carrier = audioCtx.createOscillator();
const modulator = audioCtx.createOscillator();
const modGain = audioCtx.createGain();
modulator.connect(modGain);
modGain.connect(carrier.frequency);
carrier.connect(audioCtx.destination);

parameters.modulator_frequency: Rate of modulation (ratio to carrier affects timbre)
parameters.modulation_depth: modGain.gain value — controls how much modulation (higher = more metallic/complex)

fm_synthesis.event_horizon_use: Metallic, bell-like tones for data sonification. Alien, otherworldly timbres for the void.

white_noise.description: Equal energy at all frequencies. Flat spectrum.
white_noise.implementation: AudioWorklet or buffer: for each sample, output = Math.random() * 2 - 1

pink_noise.description: Energy decreases 3dB per octave. Sounds more natural than white.
pink_noise.implementation: Apply 1/f filter to white noise (Voss-McCartney algorithm or Paul Kellet's filter)
pink_noise.use: Ocean/wind sound, calming background, natural-sounding texture

brown_noise.description: Energy decreases 6dB per octave. Deep, rumbling.
brown_noise.implementation: Integrate white noise (running sum with decay): sample = lastSample * 0.99 + random * 0.02
brown_noise.use: Deep rumble, earthquake, volcanic sounds, black hole ambient

noise_generation.event_horizon_use: Brown noise for deep space rumble. Filtered white noise for solar wind. Pink noise for atmospheric texture.

panner_node.description: Position audio sources in 3D space relative to the listener

equalpower.description: Simple left-right panning using equal-power law
equalpower.quality: Adequate for most web audio. No HRTF processing.

HRTF.description: Head-Related Transfer Function — simulates how human ears perceive 3D sound
HRTF.cost: Higher — convolves with HRTF impulse responses
HRTF.quality: Much more immersive — sounds truly appear to come from specific 3D positions
HRTF.requirement: Browser must have HRTF data loaded (usually built-in)

properties.positionX/Y/Z: Position of the sound source in 3D space
properties.orientationX/Y/Z: Direction the sound is pointing (for directional sources)
properties.refDistance: Distance at which volume starts to decrease
properties.rolloffFactor: How quickly sound fades with distance

listener.description: audioCtx.listener represents the user's ears in 3D space
listener.synchronization: Update listener position to match the camera position in the 3D scene each frame

linear.character: Predictable, steady falloff. Easy to control.
linear.use: When you need precise control over audible range

inverse.character: Rapid falloff near source, slow falloff at distance. Most realistic.
inverse.use: Default — best for most spatial audio scenarios

exponential.character: Very rapid falloff. Sound drops off dramatically.
exponential.use: When sources should only be audible very close

event_horizon_spatial_audio.black_hole_sound: Position the black hole's ambient sound at the center of the 3D scene. As scroll approaches, the spatial position stays fixed but gets 'closer' — increasing in volume and bass.
event_horizon_spatial_audio.accretion_disk: Multiple sound sources orbiting in 3D space — panned left/right as they orbit. Creates immersive rotating sound.
event_horizon_spatial_audio.approach_direction: Listener (camera) moves toward the sound source — natural Doppler-like intensity increase.

biquad_filter.description: Versatile filter for shaping frequency content

lowpass.description: Allows frequencies below cutoff. Attenuates highs.

highpass.description: Allows frequencies above cutoff. Attenuates lows.

bandpass.description: Allows frequencies around center. Attenuates both extremes.

allpass.description: Passes all frequencies but changes phase.

parameters.Q: Quality factor — narrowness of the filter. Higher Q = narrower/more resonant.
parameters.gain: Boost/cut in dB (only for shelf and peaking types)

convolver.description: Applies reverb or acoustic space simulation using an impulse response (IR)
implementation: const convolver = audioCtx.createConvolver();
const response = await fetch('impulse-response.wav');
const buffer = await audioCtx.decodeAudioData(await response.arrayBuffer());
convolver.buffer = buffer;
impulse_response_options: Real recorded IRs from famous spaces (cathedrals, studios, halls)
Very short IRs for metallic/resonant effects
Very long IRs (5-10s) for infinite space reverb
convolver.event_horizon_use: Long reverb tail for deep space ambiance. Cathedral-like reverb for transcendent moments. Metallic short reverb for HUD sounds.

dynamics_compressor.description: Reduces dynamic range — prevents audio from getting too loud while maintaining quieter sounds

parameters.threshold: dB level above which compression activates (default: -24)
parameters.knee: dB range around threshold for soft/hard compression (default: 30)
parameters.attack: Seconds for compressor to respond to increase (default: 0.003)
parameters.release: Seconds for compressor to release after decrease (default: 0.25)

dynamics_compressor.use: Master bus protection — prevent clipping when multiple sounds play simultaneously
dynamics_compressor.event_horizon_use: Essential on the master output to prevent distortion during the crescendo/crossing moment

wave_shaper.description: Non-linear distortion using a transfer function curve
wave_shaper.implementation: const shaper = audioCtx.createWaveShaper(); shaper.curve = makeCurve(); shaper.oversample = '4x';
wave_shaper.event_horizon_use: Subtle saturation on the black hole drone for warmth. Aggressive distortion during the crossing moment.

delay.description: Delays audio signal by a specified time

derived_effects.echo: Delay with feedback (output → delay → output loop via gain node)
derived_effects.flanging: Very short delay (1-10ms) modulated by LFO
derived_effects.chorus: Short delay (20-50ms) modulated by LFO, mixed with original

delay.event_horizon_use: Long echo/delay for deep space ambiance. Modulated delay for temporal distortion effects.

stereo_panner.event_horizon_use: Pan accretion disk sounds left/right as they orbit. Pan subtle ambient layers for width.

shepard_tone.description: Auditory illusion of an endlessly ascending or descending pitch. Overlapping octaves with carefully managed amplitude envelopes create the perception of infinite pitch change.
implementation: Create N oscillators (typically 6-10) spanning multiple octaves
Each oscillator's frequency continuously shifts up (or down)
As an oscillator exits the audible range at the top, fade it out
Simultaneously, a new oscillator fades in at the bottom of the range
The result: continuous ascent (or descent) that never resolves
Frequency: f_i = f_base × 2^(i/N + t × speed) where t is time

parameters.amplitude_envelope: Gaussian or cosine bell centered on mid-frequency. Edges (low/high) are silent.
parameters.speed: Typically 0.5-2 octaves per minute for subtle effect

shepard_tone.event_horizon_use: Descending Shepard tone during the approach to the event horizon — creates perpetual falling sensation. One of the most powerful audio tools for the experience.

binaural_beats.description: Two slightly different frequencies in each ear create a perceived beat at the difference frequency
implementation: Create two oscillators at slightly different frequencies (e.g., 200Hz left, 210Hz right)
Pan one fully left, one fully right
Listener perceives a 10Hz 'beat' (difference frequency)
REQUIRES HEADPHONES — does not work with speakers

frequency_ranges.delta_1_4Hz: Deep sleep, unconscious — too low for practical use

binaural_beats.caveat: Scientific evidence for cognitive effects is mixed. The SUBJECTIVE experience of binaural beats is real and can be unsettling.
binaural_beats.event_horizon_use: Low theta (4-6Hz) binaural beats during meditation/contemplation sections. Creates uneasy, altered-state feeling. MUST inform user headphones recommended.

drone_pads.description: Sustained, evolving tonal background — the foundation of ambient sound design
technique: 3-5 detuned oscillators at near-same frequency (±1-3Hz detune)
Slow amplitude modulation (0.1-0.5Hz) on each oscillator independently
Low-pass filter with slowly moving cutoff (sweep over 10-30 seconds)
Long reverb tail (ConvolverNode with 3-8 second IR)
Subtle pitch drift (oscillator frequency slowly wanders ±5Hz)

detuning_for_richness.description: Multiple oscillators at nearly the same frequency create beating patterns (chorusing)
detuning_for_richness.result: Rich, slowly evolving texture that feels alive

drone_pads.event_horizon_use: Foundation of the entire audio experience. Deep drone for the black hole (55-110Hz). Higher drone for cosmic background (220-440Hz). Evolve parameters with scroll position.

granular_synthesis.description: Decompose audio into tiny grains (10-100ms) and rearrange/overlap them to create new textures
granular_synthesis.implementation: AudioWorklet that reads from a buffer, extracting random or sequential grains with windowing, overlap, and pitch shift

parameters.position_variation: Random read position in source buffer

granular_synthesis.event_horizon_use: Granular processing of the drone during the crossing — sounds decomposing, reality fracturing

data_sonification.description: Map data values to audio parameters

mappings.audio: Base frequency of drone (higher mass = lower pitch)

gravitational_waves.description: Sonify gravitational waves — the actual 'sound' of spacetime vibrating
gravitational_waves.reference: LIGO's gravitational wave sonification of GW150914 (first detection)
gravitational_waves.implementation: Frequency chirp: f(t) increases exponentially as two black holes merge
gravitational_waves.event_horizon_use: Play during key moments — the universe itself vibrating

node_count.rule: Minimize total node count. Each node has overhead even when not producing sound.

guidelines.caution: > 100 active nodes — may cause audio glitches on mobile
guidelines.danger: > 200 active nodes — likely to cause issues

offline_audio_context.description: Pre-render audio buffers at faster-than-realtime for use later
offline_audio_context.implementation: const offlineCtx = new OfflineAudioContext(2, sampleRate * duration, sampleRate);
offline_audio_context.use: Pre-render complex reverbs, noise textures, or one-shot effects during loading
offline_audio_context.advantage: Zero runtime cost — the audio is just a buffer playback

audio_param_automation.description: Schedule parameter changes on the audio thread — more efficient and precise than setting from JS

methods.setValueAtTime: Set exact value at exact time
methods.linearRampToValueAtTime: Smooth linear interpolation to value by time
methods.exponentialRampToValueAtTime: Smooth exponential interpolation (CANNOT reach 0 — use very small value like 0.001)
methods.setTargetAtTime: Exponential approach to target with time constant
methods.setValueCurveAtTime: Follow an array of values over time
methods.cancelScheduledValues: Cancel all scheduled changes after a time

audio_param_automation.best_practice: Use automation methods instead of setting .value in requestAnimationFrame. Automation runs on the audio thread at audio rate (44100Hz) — infinitely smoother than 60Hz JS updates.

garbage_collection_prevention.description: Keep references to all audio nodes. If a node is GC'd while connected, audio may glitch.
garbage_collection_prevention.rule: Store all active nodes in an array or Map. Remove references only after disconnecting.

issues: webkitAudioContext may be needed (check: window.AudioContext || window.webkitAudioContext)
AudioContext.resume() may not work reliably — create context inside user gesture handler
OfflineAudioContext has different parameter order in older Safari
AudioWorklet support added in Safari 14.1 (2021) — older versions need ScriptProcessorNode fallback
Safari on iOS has a single audio session — your audio competes with music apps
safari_quirks.workaround: Always test on real iOS Safari. The simulator does not accurately reproduce audio behavior.

ios_audio_unlock.description: iOS requires a user touch event to unlock audio playback
ios_audio_unlock.note: Play a silent buffer on first touch to unlock the audio session

chrome_autoplay_policy.description: Chrome blocks audio autoplay until user interaction
chrome_autoplay_policy.detection: audioCtx.state === 'suspended' after creation indicates autoplay block
chrome_autoplay_policy.solution: Show UI prompting user to click/tap. Resume context on interaction.
chrome_autoplay_policy.best_ux: Integrate the unlock into the experience's 'Start' button — user clicks to begin, which also unlocks audio

issues: AudioContext constructor may throw if too many contexts exist
Different default sample rate (may be 44100 or 48000 depending on system)
AudioWorklet implementation may have subtle timing differences

sub_bass_20_60Hz.description: Felt more than heard. Physical vibration in the chest.
sub_bass_20_60Hz.implementation: Low sine oscillator (30-50Hz) with gain envelope. Use DynamicsCompressor to prevent clipping.
sub_bass_20_60Hz.event_horizon_use: The black hole's fundamental frequency. Always present, growing with proximity.

low_100_300Hz.description: Warmth and body of sound. The 'weight' of bass.
low_100_300Hz.implementation: Drone pads, low-pass filtered noise, detuned oscillators
low_100_300Hz.event_horizon_use: Background warmth for accretion disk. Reduces during void moments.

mid_300_2000Hz.description: Where most instruments and voices live. The most perceptually important range.
mid_300_2000Hz.implementation: Melodic elements, primary tones, voice (if any)
mid_300_2000Hz.event_horizon_use: Data sonification tones, Shepard tone carrier frequencies

high_mid_2000_6000Hz.description: Definition and clarity. Can be fatiguing if excessive.
high_mid_2000_6000Hz.implementation: Filtered noise bursts, high harmonics, metallic tones
high_mid_2000_6000Hz.event_horizon_use: HUD interaction sounds, high-tension moments, alarm-like tones near event horizon

high_6000_20000Hz.description: Air, sparkle, brilliance. Creates 'space' in the mix.
high_6000_20000Hz.implementation: Filtered white noise, very high harmonics, shimmer reverb
high_6000_20000Hz.event_horizon_use: Starfield shimmer, cosmic ambiance, post-crossing emergence

silence.description: The most powerful tool in the sound designer's arsenal
principles: Silence only works after establishing sound — it's the ABSENCE that creates impact
1-3 seconds of absolute silence creates maximum psychological impact
The brain continues processing the 'ghost' of the absent sound for ~500ms
Return from silence should be gentle — a sudden loud sound after silence is jarring (unless intentional for jump scare)
Partial silence (reducing to just one quiet element) can be more unsettling than total silence

implementation.total_silence: audioCtx.suspend() for true silence. Or masterGain.gain.setValueAtTime(0, time).

silence.event_horizon_use: The crossing moment: audio crescendo peaks → hard cut to absolute silence for 1.5-2 seconds. This is the single most important audio moment in the experience.

frequency_masking.description: Louder sounds at similar frequencies mask quieter ones. Important for mix clarity.
rules: Don't layer multiple sounds in the same frequency range at the same time
Use EQ (BiquadFilterNode) to carve space for each sound
The sub-bass drone should have no competition — it owns the 20-80Hz range exclusively
Data sonification tones should occupy a different frequency range than the ambient drone

sources.title: Web Audio API: Advanced Sound for Games and Interactive Apps
sources.author: Boris Smus
